title: pilosella calomastix subsp. acrosticta
intro: Pilosella calomastix subsp. acrosticta, also known as the sticky mouse-ear, is a rare plant species that belongs to the dandelion family (Asteraceae). It is traditionally used for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating wounds, ulcers, and skin irritations, due to its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, fighting infection, and promoting wound healing. Pilosella calomastix subsp. acrosticta is typically prepared as a herbal infusion or tincture, which can be used topically to treat skin conditions or ingested to support overall health and well-being.
